prose:
Benzene frontier-orbital energies computed with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') using PySCF, in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral closed-shell RKS). The geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file as-is (single point — not re-optimized at this level). SCF converged in 8 cycles with no warnings. All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true).

HOMO (index 20): −7.0468329 eV
LUMO (index 21): −0.41574728 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap: 6.6310857 eV (headline result)

The HOMO and HOMO−1 (−7.0473344 eV) are essentially degenerate (~0.5 meV apart), consistent with benzene's D6h symmetry and the doubly-degenerate e1g π HOMO.

Koopmans-theorem derived quantities:
  Vertical IP:         7.0468 eV
  Vertical EA:         0.4157 eV
  Electronegativity:   3.7313 eV
  Chemical hardness:   3.3155 eV
  Chemical softness:   0.3016 eV⁻¹
  Electrophilicity:    2.0996 eV

Total electronic energy: −232.33724 Hartree (−6322.2183 eV; for reproducibility only — not comparable across methods).

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard [input.xyz]
Output: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpx28djvf3/live.json
No warnings reported (clean run).
